description: Demo data for featcat — realistic feature derivation patterns across three sources (device_logs, client_logs, demand_v2). Populated by `featcat demo seed`; removed by `featcat demo clear`.

features.name: client_logs.error_rate_1h
features.description: Per-session error rate in the trailing hour.

features.name: client_logs.bytes_received
features.description: Bytes received by client per session.

docs.short_description: Rolling 30-day mean of CPU usage per device.
docs.long_description: Computed as a daily rolling mean over cpu_usage_raw. Smooths short bursts so sustained load shifts surface; resets to NaN after long device outages and recovers over the next 30 days.
docs.potential_issues: Devices with sparse telemetry produce noisy averages; the rolling window assumes daily uptime, so brief outages skew toward zero.

docs.short_description: Composite demand signal combining peak load and effective capacity.
docs.long_description: Defined as peak_load divided by the region's effective capacity over the same window. Capacity is recomputed weekly from the active hardware inventory; jumps after capacity additions usually reflect inventory updates, not real demand drops.
docs.expected_range: 0.0 - 1.0, with sustained values above 0.85 considered stressed.
docs.potential_issues: Stale capacity tables produce phantom drops after hardware refresh weeks; cross-check with the inventory pipeline before reacting to large step changes.

groups.name: device_health_v1
groups.description: Core signals for device-level health scoring.

groups.name: session_quality_v1
groups.description: Per-session quality signals for the network team.

groups.name: demand_forecasting
groups.description: Forecast inputs used by capacity planning.
